nodes.name: Microspingus
nodes.summary: Microspingus（Microspingus）是图谱中的分类级分类节点，当前关联8种鸟类，如Plain-tailed warbling finch、Rusty-browed warbling finch、Buff-throated warbling finch等8种鸟类。

nodes.name: Plain-tailed warbling finch
nodes.summary: The plain-tailed warbling finch (Microspingus alticola) is a species of bird in the family Thraupidae. It is endemic to Peru. Its natural habitat is subtropical or tropical moist montane forests. It is threatened by habitat loss. Taxonomy The plain-tailed warbling finch was described in 1895 by English ornithologist Osbert Salvin from a specimen collected by Oscar Theodor Baron near the town of Huamachuco in La Libertad, Peru. He originally described it in the genus Poospiza, but it was later moved to Microspingus. It is monotypic. Description The plain-tailed warbling finch is a small bird weighing 24-28g. It has a grey back, wings, and tail, a white breast and belly with tawny flanks and undertail covert, a rufous malar stripe, a white moustache and supercilium, variably yellow lores, and a black cheek and crown with a slaty cap. References
nodes.shortSummary: The plain-tailed warbling finch (Microspingus alticola) is a species of bird in the family Thraupidae. It is endemic to Peru.
